summary: o3forms hijacks common dev-tool CLI names and pulls install-time code from a GitHub commit
details: The package presents itself as an OpenMRS 'o3forms' utility but its package.json declares bin entries for webpack, webpackcli, vite, eslint, turbo, tsc, tsnode, next, jest, prettier, and nodemon — all pointing at the same trivial index.js. When hoisted into node_modules/.bin, invocations of these widely-used developer CLIs resolve to this stub, silently breaking builds and giving the publisher a foothold to ship arbitrary code under those bin names in a future version. package.json also declares an optionalDependency `stockmanagement-utils` resolved as `github:core-modules-lab/stockmanagement-utils#a8e4749` — an out-of-registry GitHub commit reference under an organization unrelated to OpenMRS, which npm will fetch and install on `npm install`, bypassing the registry's published-tarball surface. index.js additionally wraps `require('o3forms-utils')` in an empty try/catch, so any sibling package by that name on the resolve path executes on require with errors swallowed. The exported functions are no-op stubs that do not match the README's advertised functionality, and the 90.0.0 version is inconsistent with a real first-party release. Together these are the lure/staging shape of a multi-package dropper, not a usable library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
